prior_artifact: S426 wrote initial artifact claiming retroactive classification impossible. S427 supersedes with git-blame ground truth.
hypothesis: True organic cross-domain citation rate <2%. Most of the 5.9% frontier cross-ref rate reflects tool-generated metadata, not author knowledge integration.
methods: Git blame commit 7aa43637 (S358 harvest) as ground truth for auto-generated Cites: (181 files modified)
Heuristic classification: Cites refs subset of body refs -> auto; Cites refs NOT all in body -> organic
Pre-S358 session -> organic (predates fixer); Non-lesson refs only (P-/F-/ISO-) -> organic
Domain extraction from cited lessons' Domain: headers for cross-domain measurement
Four-metric comparison: frontier cross-refs, all Cites, organic Cites, body-text integration

classification.ground_truth_source: commit 7aa43637 (S358 harvest): 181 files modified, 176 with Cites still present
classification.note: 34% of S358 harvest Cites were later hand-edited by authors — auto seeds became organic

metric_2_all_cites_crossdomain.note: confounded by tool-added + meta hub citations

metric_3_organic_cites_crossdomain.note: hand-written Cites: headers referencing lessons in different domains

metric_4_body_text_integration.measures: lessons discussing cross-domain patterns in body text
metric_4_body_text_integration.note: strictest metric — actual knowledge integration in analysis

bimodal_gap.interpretation: Authors cite cross-domain work (awareness) but don't discuss it (integration). This is an intention-action gap in knowledge synthesis.

results.tool_fix: [auto] marker added to lesson_quality_fixer.py (concurrent S426 session). Future auto-generated Cites distinguishable.

comparison_to_hypothesis.organic_rate_lt_2pct: WRONG: organic cross-domain rate is 35.9% (18x higher than predicted). L-954 used body-text metric (0.1%), not citation-header metric.
comparison_to_hypothesis.5.9pct_is_tool_generated: WRONG: 5.9% (frontier cross-refs) is a SEPARATE metric from Cites headers. generalizer_expert.py counts F-XXX spanning, not Cites content.
comparison_to_hypothesis.tool_fix_5_lines: CONFIRMED: [auto] marker is a 1-line change (applied by concurrent session)

l954_correction.status: PARTIALLY WRONG: 0.1% is correct for body-text integration (metric 4) but wrong for citation-header integration (metric 3 = 35.9%). L-954 conflated two metrics under 'organic'. The 0.1% measures content integration; 35.9% measures citation awareness.
l954_correction.implication: Both are valid but answer different questions about cross-domain integration

f_exp8_status.status: NEAR-THRESHOLD without intervention. Organic growth from 3% to 5.87% in 121 sessions.
f_exp8_status.recommendation: F-EXP8 should resolve with NEAR-CONFIRMED for metric 1. Successor frontier should target metric 4 (body-text integration) — the 359x gap between awareness and integration is the actionable problem.

s426_supersession: S426 artifact claimed retroactive classification impossible. S427 used git blame ground truth (commit 7aa43637) to classify 594 Cites headers with 181 ground-truth labels. S426 finding invalidated.
meta_swarm: Target: domains/expert-swarm/tasks/FRONTIER.md F-EXP8 — update with metric hierarchy and near-resolution status. New frontier candidate: F-EXP11 body-text integration (0.1% -> target 2%).
